{"step_by_step_thinking": "The documents clearly state that fundal pressure should be avoided during shoulder dystocia maneuvers as it does not help with the delivery of the fetal shoulders and may worsen the shoulder impaction and increase the risk of uterine rupture. On the other hand, the McRoberts maneuver, suprapubic pressure, and Woods maneuver are all mentioned as effective maneuvers in the management of shoulder dystocia. Therefore, fundal pressure is the exception.", "answer_choice": "A"}
